Facts.text: Muslim forces led by Khalid ibn al-Walid capture Damascus, 1st major city of Eastern Roman Empire taken by the Rashidun Caliphate
Wamba is elected and crowned ruler of the Visigoth kingdom of Hispania in Toledo, after the death of King Recceswinth
Five Spanish ships sink in storm off Tampa, about 600 die
Perpignan surrenders to French troops
First inn opens in New France, a pastry shop and hostelry in Québec owned by Jacques Boisdon
Treaty of Harford signed establishing border between New Netherlands and New England, negotiated between Petrus Stuyvesant and Connecticut colony Governor Edward Hopkins
Treaty of Labiau: Sweden cedes Brandenburg to Prussia
Brandenburg and Poland sign the Treaty of Wehlau
Rebels under Nathaniel Bacon set Jamestown, Virginia, on fire
Giles Corey (71) is taken to an open field and pressed to death under heavy stones after refusing to recognize the authority of Salem witch trial's Court of Oyer and Terminer
Great Britain & Russia sign military agreement
Battle of Freeman's Farm (Bemis Heights) or 1st Battle of Saratoga
The Continental Congress passes the first budget of the United States
Tula, leader of Curacao slave opposition, imprisoned
Ephraim Morris granted US patent for a railroad brake
Hyperion, moon of Saturn - the first non-round moon - discovered by William Cranch Bond, his son George Phillips Bond and William Lassell
1st commercial laundry established, in Oakland, California
Henry Meyer patents sleeping rail car
-20] US Civil War: Battle at Blackford's Ford, Virginia begins
Battle at Iuka Mississippi (1,700 casualties)
Third Battle of Winchester, Virginia [Opequon], large bloody battle with the Union army defeating Confederate force with over 9,000 casualties
Atlanta University founded
Siege of Paris by Prussian forces begins (lasts until January 28, 1871)
First carpet sweeper patented by Melville Bissell of Grand Rapids, Michigan
Talks begin to set up a football club in Ottawa
The Blackpool Illuminations are switched on for the first time.
Thomas Ray becomes youngest to break a world track & field record pole-vaulting 11' 2½" at age 17 years & 198 days
One of the first known modern beauty contests is held in Spa, Belgium
Turkish frigate "Ertogrul" crashes off of coast of Japan, kills 540
Beginning of the Bombay plague epidemic when Dr.Acacio Gabriel Viegas detects the first case in Mandvi. Goes on to spread and kill 12 million in India.
11 baseball games canceled due to funeral of President William McKinley
Gen Nogi's assault on Port Arthur: 16,000 Japanese casualties
Red Tuesday; 20,000 Spanish protest for universal rights
Pius X encyclical Singular quadam, against interconfess unions
Soccer team NAC (Noad Advendo Combination) forms in Breda
The wattle is declared Australia's national flower and incorporated into the Commonwealth Coat of Arms
Brooklyn Tip-Tops' Ed Lafitte no-hits Kansas City Packers (Federal League), 6-2
Belgian troops conquer Tabora, German East Africa
First landing at Schiphol Airport, Netherlands (Farman F-22 of Soesterberg)
Ernst Tollers play "Hinkemann," premieres in Leipzig
80,000 demonstrate for democratic peace in Hague
The San Siro is inaugurated with a match between AC Milan and Inter.
Latvia dictator A Woldemaras chased out
Japanese troops conquer Mukden, South Manchuria
Lefty Grove wins his 30th game of season over White Sox, 2-1
NY Giants clinch the pennant
Bruno Haptmann arrested for kidnapping Lindbergh baby

Seven convicts take Folsom Prison Warden Clarence Larkin hostage in escape attempt, the warden, a guard, and 2 inmates are killed in ensuing stand-off; 5 remaining prisoners convicted of murder and executed by newly introduced gas chamber in California
British Expeditionary Force reaches France
Wehrmacht (German regular army) murders 100 Jews in Lukov Poland
Nazi decree forbids gentile woman to work in Jewish homes
German army conquers Kiev
Nazis force German Jews, 6 & over to wear Jewish stars
Fanny Whiteers-Koen breaks jumping world record
Liberator bombers sinks U-341
Armistice between Finland and Soviet Union is signed. (End of the Continuation War)
Luftwaffe bombs Eindhoven: 200 killed
Lord Haw Haw (William Joyce) sentenced to death in London
"Late Spring", Japanese film directed by Yasujirō Ozu, starring Chishū Ryū, Setsuko Hara and Haruko Sugimura, is released in Japan
European Payment Union forms in Paris
Great Three (England, US, and France) acknowledge Bonn Government as the only German government
UN reject membership of China's People Republic
1st broadcast of "Search for Tomorrow" on CBS-TV
Italian civil servants strike for pay increase
Hurricane Hilda, kills 200 in Mexico
1st international conference of black writers & artists meets (Sorbonne)
1st underground nuclear explosion at Las Vegas Nevada
Dalida is the 1st artist to be awarded a gold record in France for 300,000 sales of "Bambino"
Betty and Barney Hill claim that they saw a mysterious craft in the sky and that it tried to abduct them.
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
Iota Phi Theta Fraternity, Inc. is founded at Morgan State University in Baltimore, Maryland.
Erhards CDU wins West German parliament elections
Mike Burke named Yankees pres
Nigerian federal army begins the "final offensive" against the secessionist state of Biafra during the Nigerian Civil War
UCLA fires professor Angela Davis for being a communist; termination was overturned in court and she was later fired for using inflammatory language
A parcel bomb sent to Israeli Embassy in London kills a diplomat
NL refuses to allow San Diego Padres move to Washington, D.C.
Pirate Radio Free America (off Cape May NJ) forms
USSR performs underground nuclear test
Indonesia sends troops to Portuguese East Timor
New York concert promoter Sid Bernstein offers $230 million to charity for a Beatles reunion; they politely decline
"Ordinary People" film directed by Robert Redford and starring Donald Sutherland,Mary Tyler Mooreand Judd Hirsch premieres (Academy Award - Best Picture, 1981)
US Titan II Inter-Continental Ballistic Missile explodes in its storage silo near Damascus, Arkansas; 1 person killed and over 20 injured, but safety features prevented thermonuclear detonation
Satellites China 10 & 11 launched into Earth orbit by B-1 rocket
Simon & Garfunkel reunite for a concert in Central Park, NYC,
New Orleans Saints 1st road shutout victory beating Chicago Bears 10-0
Streetcars stop running on Market St, San Francisco after 122 years of service
David Slowinski on 2 CRAY-1 comp's find 2^132049-1 prime #
St Kitts & Nevis declares independence from the United Kingdom
Britain & China complete a proposed agreement to transfer Hong Kong to China by 1997
Magnitude 8.0 earthquake rocks Mexico City, killing an estimated 10,000 people and leaving 250,000 homeless
Australian cricketer Dean Jones scores 210 v India at Madras
Chicago White Sox Joe Crowley no-hits California Angels, 7-1
US Federal health officials announce the first antiretroviral medication (AZT) to prevent and treat HIV/AIDS will be available to patients
Australian swimmer Duncan Armstrong sets world record 1:47.25 to win the 200m freestyle gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; beats Anders Holmertz of Sweden by 0.64
British swimmer Adrian Moorehouse wins 100m breaststroke gold medal in 1:02.04 at the Seoul Olympics; edges Károly Güttler of Hungary by 0.01
Israel launches 1st satellite, for secret military reconnaissance

US women's all-round gymnastics team is controversially penalised 0.5 after compulsory round at the Seoul Olympics; substitute an alternate athlete on uneven bars medal podium; Americans finish 4th just 0.3 behind East Germany
Appeals court restores America's Cup to US after NY Supreme Court gave it to NZ (NZ protested US's use of a catamaran)
Chase Manhattan Discovery Center at Brooklyn Botanic Garden opens
French DC-10 crashes near Niger, 171 die
Colt Precious Bunny wins the 46th Little Brown Jug at Delaware, Ohio
Ötzi the Iceman, a five-thousand-year-old mummy, is discovered by German tourists in the Italian Alps, the oldest known natural human mummy in Europe

Demolition begins of JFK Stadium in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; built in 1926 as Sesquicentennial Stadium, also known as Municipal Stadium, it hosted the 1926 Tunney-Dempsey fight, 41 Army-Navy football games, The Beatles; The Rolling Stones; victory rallies for The NHL Flyers and MLB Phillies; and the US portion of Live-Aid
Sergei Boebka pole vaults world record (6.13m)
UN Security Council votes 12-0 (3 abstentions) to dump Yugoslavia
Actress Michele Phillips (Knots Landing) is robbed at gunpoint
Parliamentary election in Poland
San Diego Chargers' kicker John Carney boots 6 field goals in an 18-17 win over Houston to set a new NFL record with 29 consecutive field goals made
3,000 US militia lands on Haiti
Swedish government of Bildt resigns
Volcanic eruption destroys the town of Rabaul on New Britain, Papua New Guinea
Colorado Rockies Andres Galarraga becomes 4th team member to hit 30 HRs in the sesaon
MLB San Diego Padres Ken Caminiti switch hits HRs in 3rd of 4 games
The Washington Post and The New York Times publish the Unabomber's manifesto
Guelb El-Kebir massacre in Algeria leaves 53 people dead
Thai military stages a coup in Bangkok. The Constitution is revoked and martial law is declared.
9 people are killed and 20 wounded by a car bombing in Peshawar, Afghanistan
Japan beats South Africa 34-32 in Brighton, England in the biggest upset in Rugby World Cup history
Aid convoy into Urum al-Kubra, Syria attacked by air strikes, UN denounces as a war crime
7.1 earthquake south east of Mexico City kills over 300
Kansas City Royal Alex Gordon hits home run #5,694, setting a MLB record for the most home runs in a season
Korean joint summit in Pyongyang agrees to limit North Korea's weapons programme and to South Korea economic cooperation
Likely US drone attack kills at least 30 and injures 28 in Nangarhar Province, Afghanistan
North America has lost 3 billion birds (29%) since 1970 according to analysis published in "Science"
Taliban suicide truck bombing kills 22 and injures 90 at a hospital in Zabul Province, southern Afghanistan
Tropical Depression Imelda hits southeast Texas and Louisiana prompting widespread evacuations
US PresidentDonald Trumpvows to swear in a new Supreme Court judge, despite the election being only 45 days away
73rd Emmy Awards: "The Crown" Best Drama, "Ted Lasso" Best Comedy, "Mare of Easttown" Best Limited Series
Body of travelogue blogger Gabby Petito found at Bridger-Teton National Forest, Wyoming
Cumbre Vieja volcano erupts on the Spanish island of La Palma, its first eruption since 1971
US apologizes for an Afghan airstrike that killed 10 civilians, including seven children
Banks in Lebanon close for three days after a series of hold-ups by people trying to obtain their own money, frozen amid the country's economic crisis
Magnitude 7.6 earthquake strikes western Mexico on exact anniversary of two previous temblors in 1985 and 2017, killing at least two people,
Nigerian authorities says over 300 people have died and 100,000 have been displaced by the worse floods in many years
Scientists discover site of the Amazon's tallest tree, an angelim vermelho at 88.5 meters (290 feet) tall and 9.9 meters (32 feet) wide, Iratapuru River Nature Reserve, northern Brazil,
BBC broadcasts documentary "Al Fayed: Predator at Harrods" in which 20 former Harrods employees say Mohamed Al Fayed raped or sexually assaulted them
CNN reports North Carolina Republican nominee for Governor Mark Robinson posted messages on a porn site ten years ago, including calling himself a "black NAZI"
Dalmatius is raised to the rank of Caesar by his uncleConstantine I
English forces underEdward the Black Princedefeat the French at the Battle of Poitiers and capture French King John II during the Hundred Years' War
Holy Roman EmperorCharles Vand England sign an anti-French covenant
George Washington's farewell address as US President is published
Battle of Chickamauga, GA (near Chattanooga) begins; Union forces retreat after a couple days of fighting
KingLeopold IIdenies Belgian cruelty in Congo
Gustav Mahler's 7th Symphony premieres in Prague, Austria-Hungary (now Czech Republic)
George M. Cohan's musical "Get-Rich-Quick Wallingford" premieres in NYC
Lord Haw-Haw (William Joyce) becomes radio host of Reichsrundfunk Berlin
Witold Pileckiis voluntarily captured and sent to Auschwitz in order to smuggle out information and start a resistance
First meeting of partisan leadersJosip Broz TitoandDraža Mihailovićin Yugoslavia
Kim Il-sungarrives in the harbor of Wonsan, Korea, after 26 years in exile in the Soviet Union
Jackie Robinsonis named 1947 "Rookie of the Year"
"Adventures of Superman" TV series starringGeorge Reevespremieres in syndication in the US
The United States barsCharlie Chaplinfrom re-entering the country after a trip to England
An army and navy revolt forcesArgentine President JuanPerón to resign and flee to Paraguay
Cubs sluggerErnie Bankshits record 5th grand slam of season
Nikita Khrushchevis denied access to Disneyland
"LIFE" magazine celebrates artist Anna Mary "Grandma" Moses' 100th birthday by putting her on their cover
MLB Detroit Tigers pitcherDenny McLain's 31st win of the season, despiteMickey Mantle's 535th career home run, at Tiger Stadium in Detroit
Sitcom "TheMary Tyler MooreShow" premieres on CBS
Frank Robinson, Angels OF, homers in record 32nd major league ballpark - Arlington Stadium (Arlington, Texas), against the Rangers
The KGB begin a large-scale operation to discredit Russian novelistAleksandr Solzhenitsynand cut his communications with Soviet dissidents
"Fawlty Towers" starringJohn Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs premieres on BBC2 in the UK

5-day MUSE (Musicians United for Safe Energy) "No Nukes" concert against nuclear power opens at Madison Square Garden, NYC; performers include:Jackson Browne; Bonnie Raitt; Crosby, Stills & Nash;Bruce Springsteenand the E Street Band;James Taylor; Carly Simon; Gil Scott-Heron, and the Doobie Brothers
34th Emmy Awards: Hill St Blue, Barney Miller,Alan Alda& Carol Kane win

US Senate holds hearings on labeling records to warn of explicit lyrics; representatives from the Parents Music Resource Center (PMRC) testify in support, and musicians Dee Snider,Frank Zappa, andJohn Denverspeak in opposition
3-D short film "Captain EO" withMichael Jacksonpremieres at Disney theme parks
American diverGreg Louganissmashes his head on diving board during the 3m springboard preliminaries at the Seoul Olympics; recovers to qualify for the final which he wins the following day
Bon Jovirelease their 4th album "New Jersey" - most top ten hits of any rock/glam metal album (5)
"Doogie Howser, M.D.", starringNeil Patrick Harrisas a teenage physician, debuts on ABC
"Janet Jackson's Rhythm Nation 1814" 4th studio album by Janet Jackson is released (Grammy Award Best Long Form Music Video 1990, Billboard Album of the Year 1990)
American gangster film "Goodfellas", directed byMartin Scorsese, starringRobert De Niro,Joe Pesci, andRay Liotta, premieres
Barry BondsjoinsWillie Mays,Howard Johnson&Ron Gantas having (2) 30-HR/30-steal MLB seasons
45th Emmy Awards:Seinfeld, Picket Fences & Ted Danson win
Tom Glavinewins 20 games in 3 straight years
Hospital based drama series "ER" premieres on NBC, created by novelist and filmmakerMichael Crichton, starringGeorge Clooney,Anthony EdwardsandNoah Wyle
David Hare's play "Skylight" opens at Royale Theater NYC, starringMichael Gambon
Ken Griffey Jr.pinch-hits his 400th home run becoming the first major league player to reach the mark as a pinch-hitter
56th Emmy Awards: The Sopranos, Arrested Development, James Spader andAllison Janneywin
NFL Oakland Raider wide receiverJerry Riceends NFL streak of 274 consecutive games with a reception in a 13-10 victory over Buffalo Bills
TV sitcom "How I Met Your Mother" premieres, starring Josh Radnor,Neil Patrick Harris,Cobie Smulders, Jason Segel andAlyson Hannigan
Greg Madduxpitches his 5,000th career inning against the San Francisco Giants
Thierry Henryscores his first goal for Barcelona in a 3–0 Champions League group stage win over Lyon
Ashton Kutcherdebuts on "Two and a Half Men", replacingCharlie Sheen
Columbia Records releases "Cheek To Cheek", a duet album of jazz standards by singersTony BennettandLady Gaga; debuts at #1 on Billboard 200, Jazz Albums, and Traditional Jazz Albums charts.
68th Emmy Awards: hosted byJimmy Kimmel, "Game of Thrones",Julia Dreyfus,Rami Malekwin
PresidentHassan Rouhaniof Iran in his UN speech criticizes US PresidentDonald Trumpfor his comments about Iran in his own UN speech
US PresidentDonald Trumpaddresses the United Nations vowing to “totally destroy North Korea” if threatens the US
RuPaulbecomes the most decorated black artist in Emmy history, winning his 11th award for "RuPaul’s Drag Race"
British monarchQueen Elizabeth IIinterred at Windsor Castle after a state funeral at Westminster Abbey in London with an unprecedented 500 world leaders in attendance

Japanese 2-way phenomShohei Ohtanibecomes first player in MLB history to hit 50 home runs and steal 50 bases in a season; slugs 3 HRs in a 10 RBI game as the LA Dodgers rout the Miami Marlins 20-4 in Miami
New Zealand becomes the first country to grant all women the right to vote
Chubby Checker's cover version of "The Twist" hits #1 on the Billboard Hot 100
Leo VI (the Wise), Byzantine Emperor (886-912) and writer (Problematica), born in Constantinople (d. 912)
Anatoliy Konkov, Ukrainian soccer midfielder (47 caps USSR; Dynamo Kyiv	193 games) and manager (Ukraine, Shakhtar Donetsk, Zenit Leningrad), born in Krasnyi Luch, Ukraine (d. 2024)
Albert IV, Duke of Austria, born in Vienna (d. 1404)
Piero di Cosimo de' Medici [Piero the Gouty], Italian ruler of Florence (1464-69), born in Florence, Republic of Florence (d. 1469)
Jacques de Saint-Luc, Flemish composer and lutenist, baptized in Ath, Dutch Republic (d. ca. 1710)
Jan Luyts, Dutch physicist and mathematician, born in Hoorn, Netherlands (d. 1721)
Henri-Jacques de Croes, Flemish composer and violinist, born in Antwerp, Belgium (d. 1786)
Charles Humphreys, American delegate to the Continental Congress, born in Haverford, Pennsylvania (d. 1786)

Johann Georg Repsold, German scientific instrument maker, astronomer, and fireman, born in Wremen, Duchy of Bremen, Holy Roman Empire (now Germany) (d. 1830)
